problem_statement: Pantry maintenance is frequent low-attention work, but the current edit sheet opens a full form even for simple stock changes. Optional fields compete with the common tasks of adjusting quantity marking an item used up or correcting a location.
in_scope: Convert pantry item editing into a quick-action first sheet
Keep name quantity unit and used-up action in the initial visible section
Move expiry location and low-stock threshold into a collapsed secondary section and improve row summaries for low or expiring state
requirements: The first sheet view supports the common quantity-correction workflow without expanding secondary fields
Used-up remains reachable from the first sheet view
Search and existing pantry filters keep their current behavior
acceptance_criteria: Optional fields are hidden behind explicit reveal by default
Low-stock and expiring state is visible in the row summary before opening the sheet
Common pantry updates require less on-screen form scanning than the current full-form sheet
risks: Over-compressing the sheet could make rare fields too hard to find if secondary labeling is weak
implementation_notes: Update src/components/PantryEditSheet.tsx and the pantry row presentation in src/app/(app)/pantry/page.tsx. Keep delete and full edit capabilities available inside the same sheet.
